text:
Degiro (ドジーロ, Dojīro) Hunter × Hunter Hunter Association Official Issue: Hunter's Guide; Character & World Official Databook (pg. 206) is a player of the game Greed Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 14, Chapter 133 

Appearance

Degiro is fat and has short brown hair. He wears a dark mustard-colored sweater. 

Personality

Degiro is known for targeting new or inexperienced players in hopes of stealing from them. He is also rather arrogant as well as cocky. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

After Gon and Killua acquire the "Sword of Truth" card, Degiro along with some other players tail them, with the intent of stealing it. Immediately after Motaricke and Arka fail to steal the card, Degiro appears and brags about how he used a spell card to gather information beforehand which would allow him to take the "Sword of Truth" card with his "Mug" card. However, Gon manages to easily snatch away the "Mug" card before Degiro can use it, but returns it and warns him that he will take it for real next time. Killua informs Degiro that he's a mere amateur compared to a professional Hunter like Gon, adding that his dependence on spell cards has made him out of practice. As the two boys walk away, Degiro yells that their tactics won't last long since they will encounter much more skilled players. 

Later, Degiro's name is mentioned in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Degiro is capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Though Degiro's name is technically given in the manga through Gon's Greed Island binder, the correspondence between the character and his name is only provided in the Hunter's Guide databook. 

Manga and Anime Differences

Degiro does not appear in the 2011 anime adaptation of the series.
